title: tool_intercept: extract_all_tool_calls performs O(N^2) buffer clones in driving loop
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/tool_intercept.rs:243-272 and src/tool_intercept.rs:295-302
## Violation
```rust
while self.has_complete_block() {
    let (tools, before, _after) = self.extract_tool_calls();
    ...
}
```

`try_extract_invoke_format` performs each iteration:

```rust
let before = self.buffer[..start_idx].to_string();
let after = self.buffer[result_end..].to_string();
self.buffer.clone_from(&after);
```

For a buffer of N bytes containing K tool calls, total allocation is O(N*K) — quadratic. `has_complete_block` re-scans end-to-end on every loop iteration on top of that.

## Mandated refactor
Single-pass parser: walk the buffer once with a cursor and slice views. At minimum, replace `self.buffer.clone_from(&after)` with `self.buffer.drain(..result_end);` which is O(N) total instead of O(N*K).
## Filed from QA review dispatch
